title: Dryopteris orientalis
intro: Dryopteris orientalis, also known as the Oriental golden shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris orientalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is often used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, pneumonia, and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Dryopteris orientalis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which comprises over 250 species of ferns. The genus Dryopteris is further classified under the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns that also includes the genera Polystichum and Athyrium. The family Dryopteridaceae is part of the class Polypodiopsida, which includes all the ferns. Dryopteris orientalis is native to Asia and is commonly found in forests and woodlands.
uses: Dryopteris orientalis, also known as the Oriental lady fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal uses include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's extract is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and swelling. parts: Dryopteris orientalis, also known as the oriental wood fern, has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in East Asian cultures. The medicinal parts of this plant include its rhizomes, leaves, and fronds, which are believed to possess various health benefits. The rhizomes of Dryopteris orientalis are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, while its leaves and fronds are thought to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of fever. preparations: Dryopteris orientalis is a plant that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. side_effects: I couldn't find specific information on the side effects of Dryopteris orientalis.
